pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates the starter notebook at this exact path
export_result.sh checks for the CSV file at this exact path
export_result.sh checks for the chart file at this exact path
setup_task.sh computes initial dataset ground truth statistics to this path
verifier.py checks exactly these 5 target strings via string matching on CSV columns in export_result.sh
verifier.py strictly enforces row_count >= min_zones in its scoring logic, and Python inspection of sanfran_public.h5 shows 188 zones with >10 households
verifier.py strictly requires a final score >= 60 to pass the task

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes the public San Francisco dataset for UrbanSim (sanfran_public.h5) from UDST, downloaded directly from their GitHub repository, making the data non-synthetic. The required CSV output (/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/displacement_risk.csv) and bar chart (/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/displacement_risk_chart.png) paths are exactly verified against export and scoring scripts. Independent analysis of the HDF5 baseline data confirms there are 189 unique zones in the dataset, easily accommodating the verifier's 50 minimum zones requirement. The required CSV columns (zone_id, dri_score, vulnerability_score, precarity_score, pressure_score) align perfectly with the substring checks scored by verifier.py. The required pass threshold of 60 out of 100 points matches the strict success constraint.
